response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable full-stack and polyglot engineer, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no exposure to fixed income, fixed income derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking systems in any technical capacity, and no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit, Kafka, or Windows platform development. The sole financially adjacent employer (RIDE Capital) used TypeScript and Node.js exclusively.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated ability to ramp up across multiple languages and platforms
Strong software craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — transferable to any engineering environment
Some financial-services-adjacent exposure through RIDE Capital, indicating baseline familiarity with regulated-industry engineering
concerns: C++ experience is minimal — approximately six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis; far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
No fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge of any kind
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle usage, no Windows platform development, and no mention of Summit, Kafka, or financial messaging infrastructure
